name: Araylyn Clements King
ai_summary: Araylyn Clements King has three years of professional childcare experience, including Georgia Pre-K paraprofessional and lead daycare teacher roles. Based in McDonough, GA (60mi), she offers full-time availability and focuses on developmentally appropriate behavior management and child-centered care.

transcripts.q3:
Hi, my name is airlin Clements King. I've been working professionally in childcare for three years now. I began as a paraprofessional in Georgia Pre-K, and then moved to become a lead daycare teacher. I have been a Lead Teacher for ages, six weeks through three years. And I have also worked, educationally with school-age children, 5 to 12 and after-school programs, including leading a small reading group for middle grade student with learning disabilities. Most recently, I have been in early intervention registered Behavior technician providing Direct
 One-on-one applied behavioral analysis therapy to children, aged 2 to 6 to her on the autism spectrum. I love the work that I do because I love the opportunity to Foster growth at such a crucial stage in development, seeing a child learn. Whether that be through academic instruction or naturalistic social engagement is incredibly rewarding.
transcripts.q4: I am currently located in McDonough, Georgia, and I'm willing to travel up to 60 miles for work.
transcripts.q5: I am open to either part or full-time positions and I have availability from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m.
transcripts.q6: I always first consider what the developmentally appropriate behaviors are when managing conflict between children. I aim for positive language and redirection to appropriate behaviors rather than simply telling the child to stop the current behavior. I also focus, and what the behavior is trying to communicate and ensure that the replacement Behavior fulfills that same function. I arranged the environment to prevent similar conflict from occurring. Such as redirecting, the children to activities away from each other.
transcripts.q7: I believe my biggest strengths in childcare are adaptability in patients. I would make a great up kid teacher because I meet the child on their level, while maintaining boundaries for health, safety and hygiene. I do also have a strong background in Center work and prioritize organization communication and following safety, standards and Regulation and childcare.
